ca-rn-nclex-rn.title: Chain of Infection
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Chain of Infection | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Chain of Infection — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Chain of Infection** supports NCLEX-RN **infection prevention and control**: you break the **chain of infection**, apply **standard precautions** to every patient, add **transmission-based** layers when indicated, and escalate **sepsis** or **exposure** concerns without delay. Canadian stems may reference **PHAC**-aligned language and **metric** vitals; US items test the same **first action** and **PPE** logic.

Boards reward **policy-faithful** sequencing—**soap and water** after **C. diff** care, **N95** and **negative pressure** for **airborne** pathogens when the stem signals it, and **rapid response** or **provider notification** when instability appears.

Link [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Learning objectives:** match **pathogen** and **route** to **precautions**; execute **hand hygiene** per indication; protect **self**, **patients**, and **visitors**.
- **Nursing priorities:** correct **PPE**; maintain **negative pressure** integrity when assigned; **cluster** care thoughtfully without delaying **urgent** needs.
- **Red flags / safety:** **doffing** errors; **wrong room** for **TB**; **continuing** alcohol-only hand hygiene after **C. diff** care when policy requires soap and water.
- **Patient teaching:** respiratory hygiene; **isolation** rationale; **when to seek care** for fever or spreading redness.
- **Clinical reasoning:** **assess → protect → notify** before low-yield tasks when instability is present.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** A hospitalized adult with concerns related to **Chain of Infection** develops **fever** and **tachycardia** during your shift. You notice **hypotension** when standing and **confusion** new for this client.

**Fork:** **Repeat vitals**, **apply oxygen** if ordered, **obtain** **lactate** and **cultures** per protocol, **notify the provider** with **SBAR**, and **initiate isolation** precautions if **source** clues suggest **transmissible** risk—not routine hygiene alone first.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

**Related:** [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice) · [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy) · [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics) · [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

preTest.question: For Chain of Infection, which action best matches NCLEX-RN infection control priorities?
options: Complete non-urgent charting before reporting suspected sepsis with hypotension
Reassess vitals and perfusion, follow sepsis/isolation protocols per orders, and notify the provider with objective data when criteria are met
Discontinue isolation without provider or infection prevention direction
Delegate unstable assessment to the nursing assistant alone
preTest.rationale: Infection-related deterioration requires reassessment, protocol-aligned interventions, and timely communication before deferrable tasks.

preTest.question: After caring for a client with suspected C. difficile diarrhea, which hand hygiene practice is most appropriate?
options: Alcohol-based hand rub alone because it is faster
Soap and water when spore-forming organism precautions are indicated per policy
Skip hand hygiene until end of shift
Only wash if visible soil is present
preTest.rationale: Clostridioides difficile spores are not reliably inactivated by alcohol-based rub alone; soap and water friction is required after care when policy indicates.

postTest.question: Which finding most urgently requires escalation in an infection control context for Chain of Infection?
options: Mild dry cough in afebrile client
MAP 58 mmHg with rising lactate and new confusion in a febrile client
Request for an extra blanket
Chronic osteoarthritis pain unchanged from baseline
postTest.rationale: Hypotension with rising lactate and altered mental status in a febrile patient suggests sepsis/septic shock until evaluated and requires immediate escalation.

postTest.question: When is detailed patient teaching about Chain of Infection most appropriate?
options: During active hemodynamic instability
After stabilization when the client is alert and able to participate
Only at home without follow-up
Before any assessment to save time
postTest.rationale: Education is most effective after acute threats are stabilized and the patient can engage safely.

ca-rn-nclex-rn.title: Standard Precautions Deep Dive
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Standard Precautions Deep Dive | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Standard Precautions Deep Dive — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Standard Precautions Deep Dive** focuses on **transmission-based precautions** layered on **standard precautions**: correct **room** engineering (negative pressure for **airborne**), **PPE** selection (**N95** vs surgical mask), **donning/doffing** without self-contamination, and **visitor**/transport discipline. NCLEX punishes **wrong mask type**, **doffing** that contaminates the face, or **cohorting** incompatible organisms.

Integrate [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [C. diff infection control](LESSON:c-diff-infection-control), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Airborne vs droplet vs contact distinctions appear every cycle.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Contact:** gloves + gown; dedicated equipment; **C. diff** → soap and water handwashing.
- **Droplet:** surgical mask within **1–2 m**; private room or cohort **same pathogen**.
- **Airborne:** fit-tested **N95** (or PAPR per policy); **AIIR**; door closed.
- **Neutropenic protective:** **HL** menus, fresh flowers avoidance themes, visitor screening per policy.
- **Transport:** mask type on patient per precaution category.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** A client is **admitted** with **productive cough**, **night sweats**, and **weight loss** while **awaiting** **TB** rule-out. They are currently in a **semi-private** bay.

**Fork:** **Airborne precautions** and **AIIR** per policy—**mask** the client for transport, **notify** infection prevention/provider, **do not** leave in **open** multi-bed space when stem implies **pulmonary TB** suspicion.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Airborne Precautions | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Airborne Precautions — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Airborne Precautions** focuses on **transmission-based precautions** layered on **standard precautions**: correct **room** engineering (negative pressure for **airborne**), **PPE** selection (**N95** vs surgical mask), **donning/doffing** without self-contamination, and **visitor**/transport discipline. NCLEX punishes **wrong mask type**, **doffing** that contaminates the face, or **cohorting** incompatible organisms.

Integrate [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [C. diff infection control](LESSON:c-diff-infection-control), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Airborne vs droplet vs contact distinctions appear every cycle.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Contact:** gloves + gown; dedicated equipment; **C. diff** → soap and water handwashing.
- **Droplet:** surgical mask within **1–2 m**; private room or cohort **same pathogen**.
- **Airborne:** fit-tested **N95** (or PAPR per policy); **AIIR**; door closed.
- **Neutropenic protective:** **HL** menus, fresh flowers avoidance themes, visitor screening per policy.
- **Transport:** mask type on patient per precaution category.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** A client is **admitted** with **productive cough**, **night sweats**, and **weight loss** while **awaiting** **TB** rule-out. They are currently in a **semi-private** bay.

**Fork:** **Airborne precautions** and **AIIR** per policy—**mask** the client for transport, **notify** infection prevention/provider, **do not** leave in **open** multi-bed space when stem implies **pulmonary TB** suspicion.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

**Related:** [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice) · [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy) · [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics) · [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

preTest.question: For Airborne Precautions, which action best matches NCLEX-RN infection control priorities?
options: Complete non-urgent charting before reporting suspected sepsis with hypotension
Reassess vitals and perfusion, follow sepsis/isolation protocols per orders, and notify the provider with objective data when criteria are met
Discontinue isolation without provider or infection prevention direction
Delegate unstable assessment to the nursing assistant alone
preTest.rationale: Infection-related deterioration requires reassessment, protocol-aligned interventions, and timely communication before deferrable tasks.

preTest.question: After caring for a client with suspected C. difficile diarrhea, which hand hygiene practice is most appropriate?
options: Alcohol-based hand rub alone because it is faster
Soap and water when spore-forming organism precautions are indicated per policy
Skip hand hygiene until end of shift
Only wash if visible soil is present
preTest.rationale: Clostridioides difficile spores are not reliably inactivated by alcohol-based rub alone; soap and water friction is required after care when policy indicates.

postTest.question: Which finding most urgently requires escalation in an infection control context for Airborne Precautions?
options: Mild dry cough in afebrile client
MAP 58 mmHg with rising lactate and new confusion in a febrile client
Request for an extra blanket
Chronic osteoarthritis pain unchanged from baseline
postTest.rationale: Hypotension with rising lactate and altered mental status in a febrile patient suggests sepsis/septic shock until evaluated and requires immediate escalation.

postTest.question: When is detailed patient teaching about Airborne Precautions most appropriate?
options: During active hemodynamic instability
After stabilization when the client is alert and able to participate
Only at home without follow-up
Before any assessment to save time
postTest.rationale: Education is most effective after acute threats are stabilized and the patient can engage safely.

ca-rn-nclex-rn.title: Transmission-Based Precautions: Which Room, Which PPE?
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Transmission-Based Precautions: Which Room, Which PPE? | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Transmission-Based Precautions: Which Room, Which PPE? — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Transmission-Based Precautions: Which Room, Which PPE?** trains **NGN-style prioritization** for infection scenarios: unstable **sepsis**, **airway** compromise with **hemoptysis**, **wrong isolation** leaving others exposed, **needlestick** reporting windows, **C. diff** outbreaks, or **post-op fever** with **rigidity** outrank stable teaching. Pick the client with **objective** high risk first.

Anchor with [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Multi-patient matrices reward **one unstable infection** client first.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Airway** and **hemodynamic** collapse beat **charting**.
- **Suspected TB** in shared space → **airborne** isolation beats finishing **admission** questions.
- **Needlestick** from **HIV-risk** source → **immediate** chain of reporting beats end-of-shift **incident** form only.
- **C. diff** outbreak → **contact** + **environmental** escalation per policy.
- **Delegate** stable tasks only after **unstable** clients covered.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** Four clients: (A) **febrile** **neutropenic** with **rigors**, (B) **stable** **isolation** teaching request, (C) **needlestick** **2 hours ago** not yet reported, (D) routine **vitals** on stable post-op.

**Fork:** **See Client A first** for **febrile neutropenia** emergency pathway; then **C** for **PEP** window; then **D**; then **B** with **clear communication**.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

ca-rn-nclex-rn.title: Healthcare-Associated Infections
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Healthcare-Associated Infections | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
ca-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Healthcare-Associated Infections — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Healthcare-Associated Infections** addresses **healthcare-associated infection (HAI)** prevention bundles: **indication** for devices, **aseptic insertion**, **daily necessity review**, **maintenance** practices (**scrub the hub**, closed **urinary** systems, **oral care** and **HOB** for ventilated clients), and **surgical** **prophylaxis** timing themes when the stem includes OR clients. Nursing judgment ties **early device removal** to risk reduction.

Pair [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [wound infection vs colonization](LESSON:wound-infection-vs-colonization), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Device bundles test **indication**, **maintenance**, and **removal**.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **CAUTI:** sterile insertion; closed drainage; bag below bladder; **daily** catheter necessity review.
- **CLABSI:** maximal sterile barrier at insertion; **scrub the hub**; assess **dressing**; remove when unnecessary.
- **VAP:** **HOB 30–45°**; oral care; **SED** vacation per orders; subglottic suction when device present.
- **SSI:** pre-op **skin** prep themes; **glycemic** control; **normothermia**; **hair removal** clippers not razors when tested.
- **Surveillance:** report **clusters** to infection prevention.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** **POD 3** post **abdominal surgery**, the client has **increasing** incision **pain**, **serosanguinous** drainage with **new** purulence, and **temperature** **38.9 °C**.

**Fork:** **Assess** wound with **sterile** technique per policy, **notify surgeon/provider**, obtain **wound culture** if ordered, **monitor** for **sepsis**—not **reinforce** dressing blindly without reporting **infection** signs.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:

- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.

**VAP Prevention** addresses **healthcare-associated infection (HAI)** prevention bundles: **indication** for devices, **aseptic insertion**, **daily necessity review**, **maintenance** practices (**scrub the hub**, closed **urinary** systems, **oral care** and **HOB** for ventilated clients), and **surgical** **prophylaxis** timing themes when the stem includes OR clients. Nursing judgment ties **early device removal** to risk reduction.

Pair [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [wound infection vs colonization](LESSON:wound-infection-vs-colonization), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Device bundles test **indication**, **maintenance**, and **removal**.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **CAUTI:** sterile insertion; closed drainage; bag below bladder; **daily** catheter necessity review.
- **CLABSI:** maximal sterile barrier at insertion; **scrub the hub**; assess **dressing**; remove when unnecessary.
- **VAP:** **HOB 30–45°**; oral care; **SED** vacation per orders; subglottic suction when device present.
- **SSI:** pre-op **skin** prep themes; **glycemic** control; **normothermia**; **hair removal** clippers not razors when tested.
- **Surveillance:** report **clusters** to infection prevention.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** **POD 3** post **abdominal surgery**, the client has **increasing** incision **pain**, **serosanguinous** drainage with **new** purulence, and **temperature** **38.9 °C**.

**Fork:** **Assess** wound with **sterile** technique per policy, **notify surgeon/provider**, obtain **wound culture** if ordered, **monitor** for **sepsis**—not **reinforce** dressing blindly without reporting **infection** signs.

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

preTest.question: For VAP Prevention, which action best matches NCLEX-RN infection control priorities?
options: Complete non-urgent charting before reporting suspected sepsis with hypotension
Reassess vitals and perfusion, follow sepsis/isolation protocols per orders, and notify the provider with objective data when criteria are met
Discontinue isolation without provider or infection prevention direction
Delegate unstable assessment to the nursing assistant alone
preTest.rationale: Infection-related deterioration requires reassessment, protocol-aligned interventions, and timely communication before deferrable tasks.

preTest.question: After caring for a client with suspected C. difficile diarrhea, which hand hygiene practice is most appropriate?
options: Alcohol-based hand rub alone because it is faster
Soap and water when spore-forming organism precautions are indicated per policy
Skip hand hygiene until end of shift
Only wash if visible soil is present
preTest.rationale: Clostridioides difficile spores are not reliably inactivated by alcohol-based rub alone; soap and water friction is required after care when policy indicates.

postTest.question: Which finding most urgently requires escalation in an infection control context for VAP Prevention?
options: Mild dry cough in afebrile client
MAP 58 mmHg with rising lactate and new confusion in a febrile client
Request for an extra blanket
Chronic osteoarthritis pain unchanged from baseline
postTest.rationale: Hypotension with rising lactate and altered mental status in a febrile patient suggests sepsis/septic shock until evaluated and requires immediate escalation.

postTest.question: When is detailed patient teaching about VAP Prevention most appropriate?
options: During active hemodynamic instability
After stabilization when the client is alert and able to participate
Only at home without follow-up
Before any assessment to save time
postTest.rationale: Education is most effective after acute threats are stabilized and the patient can engage safely.

sections.body:

Expect **hand hygiene** method traps (**soap and water** for **C. diff** spores).

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:

sections.heading: Clinical meaning

**Antibiotic Stewardship** ties **antimicrobial stewardship** to nursing practice: **cultures before antibiotics** when feasible and the patient is not crashing, **indication** review, **de-escalation** when sensitivities return, and **adverse effect** monitoring. NCLEX rewards **not delaying** antibiotics in **septic shock** while still prioritizing **culture discipline** when safe.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Obtain cultures** before first dose when **safe** and patient not in **immediate** collapse.
- **Renal** dose adjustments when stem provides **CrCl**.
- **Narrow** therapy when sensitivities return.
- **Duration** stewardship for uncomplicated UTIs when item frames outpatient care.
- **Teach** completion of prescribed courses for susceptible infections when appropriate.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Wound Infection, Colonization, and Dehiscence** differentiates **colonization** from **true infection**, recognizes **dehiscence** and **evisceration** red flags, and reinforces **aseptic** dressing technique and **culture** of **tissue** or **purulent** material when ordered—not swabbing **non-purulent** chronic wounds inappropriately. Infection control overlaps with **contact** precautions for **draining** wounds with resistant organisms.

Pair [wound infection vs colonization](LESSON:wound-infection-vs-colonization), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. **Evisceration** → **cover**, **NPO**, **notify**, **prepare** OR—classic sequence.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Colonization** may show positive swab without **clinical** infection signs.
- **Infection:** increasing pain, **purulence**, spreading **erythema**, fever.
- **Dehiscence:** wound **edges** separate; **evisceration** is **emergent**—cover with **sterile** saline-moist dressing per policy, **NPO**, **notify**.
- **Dressing:** **aseptic** technique; **wound** isolation when **MDRO** drainage.
- **Photograph**/measure per policy—not assumed scope.

sections.body:

**Fork:** **Cover** with **sterile** saline-moist dressing per policy, **NPO**, **call for help**, **notify surgeon** immediately—**classic evisceration** emergency sequence.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Patient Placement and Cohorting** focuses on **transmission-based precautions** layered on **standard precautions**: correct **room** engineering (negative pressure for **airborne**), **PPE** selection (**N95** vs surgical mask), **donning/doffing** without self-contamination, and **visitor**/transport discipline. sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Airborne vs droplet vs contact distinctions appear every cycle.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Contact:** gloves + gown; dedicated equipment; **C. diff** → soap and water handwashing.
- **Droplet:** surgical mask within **1–2 m**; private room or cohort **same pathogen**.
- **Airborne:** fit-tested **N95** (or PAPR per policy); **AIIR**; door closed.
- **Neutropenic protective:** **HL** menus, fresh flowers avoidance themes, visitor screening per policy.
- **Transport:** mask type on patient per precaution category.

us-rn-nclex-rn.title: Hand Hygiene and Aseptic Technique
us-rn-nclex-rn.seoTitle: Hand Hygiene and Aseptic Technique | NCLEX-RN | NurseNest
us-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Hand Hygiene and Aseptic Technique — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Hand Hygiene and Aseptic Technique** supports NCLEX-RN **infection prevention and control**: you break the **chain of infection**, apply **standard precautions** to every patient, add **transmission-based** layers when indicated, and escalate **sepsis** or **exposure** concerns without delay. Canadian stems may reference **PHAC**-aligned language and **metric** vitals; US items test the same **first action** and **PPE** logic.

Boards reward **policy-faithful** sequencing—**soap and water** after **C. diff** care, **N95** and **negative pressure** for **airborne** pathogens when the stem signals it, and **rapid response** or **provider notification** when instability appears.

Link [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Learning objectives:** match **pathogen** and **route** to **precautions**; execute **hand hygiene** per indication; protect **self**, **patients**, and **visitors**.
- **Nursing priorities:** correct **PPE**; maintain **negative pressure** integrity when assigned; **cluster** care thoughtfully without delaying **urgent** needs.
- **Red flags / safety:** **doffing** errors; **wrong room** for **TB**; **continuing** alcohol-only hand hygiene after **C. diff** care when policy requires soap and water.
- **Patient teaching:** respiratory hygiene; **isolation** rationale; **when to seek care** for fever or spreading redness.
- **Clinical reasoning:** **assess → protect → notify** before low-yield tasks when instability is present.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** A hospitalized adult with concerns related to **Hand Hygiene and Aseptic Technique** develops **fever** and **tachycardia** during your shift. You notice **hypotension** when standing and **confusion** new for this client.

**Fork:** **Repeat vitals**, **apply oxygen** if ordered, **obtain** **lactate** and **cultures** per protocol, **notify the provider** with **SBAR**, and **initiate isolation** precautions if **source** clues suggest **transmissible** risk—not routine hygiene alone first.

- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Transmission-Based Precautions: Which Room, Which PPE?** trains **NGN-style prioritization** for infection scenarios: unstable **sepsis**, **airway** compromise with **hemoptysis**, **wrong isolation** leaving others exposed, **needlestick** reporting windows, **C. diff** outbreaks, or **post-op fever** with **rigidity** outrank stable teaching. Pick the client with **objective** high risk first.

Anchor with [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Multi-patient matrices reward **one unstable infection** client first.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Airway** and **hemodynamic** collapse beat **charting**.
- **Suspected TB** in shared space → **airborne** isolation beats finishing **admission** questions.
- **Needlestick** from **HIV-risk** source → **immediate** chain of reporting beats end-of-shift **incident** form only.
- **C. diff** outbreak → **contact** + **environmental** escalation per policy.
- **Delegate** stable tasks only after **unstable** clients covered.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario

**Fork:** **See Client A first** for **febrile neutropenia** emergency pathway; then **C** for **PEP** window; then **D**; then **B** with **clear communication**.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Healthcare-Associated Infections** addresses **healthcare-associated infection (HAI)** prevention bundles: **indication** for devices, **aseptic insertion**, **daily necessity review**, **maintenance** practices (**scrub the hub**, closed **urinary** systems, **oral care** and **HOB** for ventilated clients), and **surgical** **prophylaxis** timing themes when the stem includes OR clients. Nursing judgment ties **early device removal** to risk reduction.

Pair [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [wound infection vs colonization](LESSON:wound-infection-vs-colonization), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Device bundles test **indication**, **maintenance**, and **removal**.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **CAUTI:** sterile insertion; closed drainage; bag below bladder; **daily** catheter necessity review.
- **CLABSI:** maximal sterile barrier at insertion; **scrub the hub**; assess **dressing**; remove when unnecessary.
- **VAP:** **HOB 30–45°**; oral care; **SED** vacation per orders; subglottic suction when device present.
- **SSI:** pre-op **skin** prep themes; **glycemic** control; **normothermia**; **hair removal** clippers not razors when tested.
- **Surveillance:** report **clusters** to infection prevention.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** **POD 3** post **abdominal surgery**, the client has **increasing** incision **pain**, **serosanguinous** drainage with **new** purulence, and **temperature** **38.9 °C**.

**Fork:** **Assess** wound with **sterile** technique per policy, **notify surgeon/provider**, obtain **wound culture** if ordered, **monitor** for **sepsis**—not **reinforce** dressing blindly without reporting **infection** signs.

preTest.question: For Surgical Site Infection Prevention, which action best matches NCLEX-RN infection control priorities?

Only at home without follow-up

postTest.rationale: Education is most effective after acute threats are stabilized and the patient can engage safely.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Antibiotic Stewardship** ties **antimicrobial stewardship** to nursing practice: **cultures before antibiotics** when feasible and the patient is not crashing, **indication** review, **de-escalation** when sensitivities return, and **adverse effect** monitoring. NCLEX rewards **not delaying** antibiotics in **septic shock** while still prioritizing **culture discipline** when safe.

Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Balance **culture timing** with **urgency** of sepsis.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Obtain cultures** before first dose when **safe** and patient not in **immediate** collapse.
- **Renal** dose adjustments when stem provides **CrCl**.
- **Narrow** therapy when sensitivities return.
- **Duration** stewardship for uncomplicated UTIs when item frames outpatient care.
- **Teach** completion of prescribed courses for susceptible infections when appropriate.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:

**Fork:** **Obtain cultures before first dose** when **feasible** per bundle—if **delay** would exceed safe window, follow **sepsis urgency** framing in the stem; document times clearly.

sections.heading: Core concept

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Wound Infection, Colonization, and Dehiscence** differentiates **colonization** from **true infection**, recognizes **dehiscence** and **evisceration** red flags, and reinforces **aseptic** dressing technique and **culture** of **tissue** or **purulent** material when ordered—not swabbing **non-purulent** chronic wounds inappropriately. Infection control overlaps with **contact** precautions for **draining** wounds with resistant organisms.

Pair [wound infection vs colonization](LESSON:wound-infection-vs-colonization), [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [sepsis early recognition](LESSON:sepsis-early-recognition-hy), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. **Evisceration** → **cover**, **NPO**, **notify**, **prepare** OR—classic sequence.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Colonization** may show positive swab without **clinical** infection signs.
- **Infection:** increasing pain, **purulence**, spreading **erythema**, fever.
- **Dehiscence:** wound **edges** separate; **evisceration** is **emergent**—cover with **sterile** saline-moist dressing per policy, **NPO**, **notify**.
- **Dressing:** **aseptic** technique; **wound** isolation when **MDRO** drainage.
- **Photograph**/measure per policy—not assumed scope.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** Abdominal **wound** **edges** **separate** with **visceral** **bulge** noted when dressing removed.

**Fork:** **Cover** with **sterile** saline-moist dressing per policy, **NPO**, **call for help**, **notify surgeon** immediately—**classic evisceration** emergency sequence.

us-rn-nclex-rn.seoDescription: NCLEX-RN infection control review: Patient Placement and Cohorting — isolation, PPE, HAI prevention, sepsis escalation, exposures, stewardship, Canada- and US-aligned clinical judgment.

sections.heading: Clinical meaning
sections.body:
**Patient Placement and Cohorting** focuses on **transmission-based precautions** layered on **standard precautions**: correct **room** engineering (negative pressure for **airborne**), **PPE** selection (**N95** vs surgical mask), **donning/doffing** without self-contamination, and **visitor**/transport discipline. NCLEX punishes **wrong mask type**, **doffing** that contaminates the face, or **cohorting** incompatible organisms.

Integrate [isolation precautions in practice](LESSON:isolation-precautions-in-practice), [PPE & transmission basics](LESSON:ppe-transmission-basics), [C. diff infection control](LESSON:c-diff-infection-control), and [Canada RN hub](/canada/rn/nclex-rn/lessons) · [US RN hub](/us/rn/nclex-rn/lessons).

sections.heading: Exam relevance
sections.body: Examiners use **first**, **priority**, and **most important** language. Eliminate answers that **delay cultures** in **stable** patients when the stem rewards culture-first discipline, or that **delay antibiotics** in **shock**. Watch **PPE** sequence, **room type**, and **hand hygiene** method mismatches. Airborne vs droplet vs contact distinctions appear every cycle.

sections.heading: Core concept
sections.body:
- **Contact:** gloves + gown; dedicated equipment; **C. diff** → soap and water handwashing.
- **Droplet:** surgical mask within **1–2 m**; private room or cohort **same pathogen**.
- **Airborne:** fit-tested **N95** (or PAPR per policy); **AIIR**; door closed.
- **Neutropenic protective:** **HL** menus, fresh flowers avoidance themes, visitor screening per policy.
- **Transport:** mask type on patient per precaution category.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:
**Patient vignette.** A client is **admitted** with **productive cough**, **night sweats**, and **weight loss** while **awaiting** **TB** rule-out. They are currently in a **semi-private** bay.

**Fork:** **Airborne precautions** and **AIIR** per policy—**mask** the client for transport, **notify** infection prevention/provider, **do not** leave in **open** multi-bed space when stem implies **pulmonary TB** suspicion.

- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.

sections.heading: Core concept

- **Needlestick** from **HIV-risk** source → **immediate** chain of reporting beats end-of-shift **incident** form only.
- **C. diff** outbreak → **contact** + **environmental** escalation per policy.
- **Delegate** stable tasks only after **unstable** clients covered.

sections.heading: Clinical scenario
sections.body:

sections.heading: Takeaways
sections.body:
- **Standard + transmission-based** layers stack—never drop standard precautions.
- **Sepsis** and **isolation** mistakes both harm—tie **vitals** to **action**.
- **Synthesis:** infection control is **everyone’s** job; **objective** escalation wins on boards.
